data.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Ramps to 18-Month High as Oil Soars
data.description:
The 10-year Treasury yield rose 5 basis points to 4.707% on Wednesday, marking its highest level since January 2025.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Rising inflation expectations caused by higher oil prices are behind the uptick. Brent crude (BZ), the global oil benchmark, is up by over 6% on the day after the Iran-backed Houthi military group attacked two Saudi tankers attempting to cross the Bab al-Mandeb Strait. The Houthis had previously imposed a maritime blockade on Saudi ports, further threatening global energy prices as traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains restricted.

Inflation Risks Keep Fed Hike Bets Alive

Higher inflation expectations could push the Fed to keep rates higher for longer. That can drive yields higher as investors adjust to a more hawkish outlook and demand additional compensation for holding government debt.

The Fed's next interest rate decision is scheduled for July 29. The central bank will likely hold rates steady, with the CME FedWatch tool assigning 62.1% odds to the scenario. However, the odds of a 25 bps rate hike have been steadily rising and are at 37.9%, up from 34.2% a day ago and 11.8% a week ago.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Will a Classic Disney Experience Keep Customers Coming Back? Disney Stock (NYSE:DIS) Drops
data.description:
Entertainment giant Disney (DIS) is in desperate need of a draw these days. Its movies are faltering, as evidenced by the lackluster performance that was Moana's live-action remake. And the parks are getting to be so expensive that many vacationers are looking to other countries for relief. But Disney knows it has a lot to work with, and is putting investment into classic experiences to draw interest. Investors seem skeptical, and sent Disney stock on a downward ride of its own, shaving off 3% in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Alec Ras, who serves as director of facility asset management, detailed how Disney is putting investment into its classic attractions to help keep a connection with customers. Ras heads a team of over 4,000 Cast Members who complete a combined total of projects annually measured in the thousands. One recent project featured a complete steel track replacement on Big Thunder Mountain Railroad, a ride that has been operating at Disney since 1979.

And the projects appear to be working. Reports note that Big Thunder Mountain Railroad's guest satisfaction scores are up significantly after its reopening. It was not alone; the Country Bear Musical Jamboree and Buzz Lightyear's Space Ranger Spin were both recently retooled, and saw their scores rise as well. This proves that the reinvestment can represent a tangible gain.

Adding Toy Story to Softball

With Toy Story 5 one of the biggest winners in Disney's stable right now, it is hardly surprising that Disney is putting the characters to work on other projects to capitalize on that positive customer sentiment. The annual championship series of Athletes Unlimited Softball League will air this Saturday on Disney's ABC, and will feature characters Woody and Jessie at the big event.

The duo will lead a pregame parade, and also throw out the first pitch at the big game. The ABC telecast will feature scenes from those side events. Athletes Unlimited's chief growth officer, Allie Kleva, noted, "Our mission right now is to grow this league, to grow our audience, to introduce professional softball and our athletes to folks across the country. Having 'Toy Story' engage with us just elevates the property."

Is Disney Stock a Buy or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on DIS stock based on 17 Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 21.39% loss in its share price over the past year, the average DIS price target of $129.94 per share implies 39.53% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: BofA Stays Bullish on Tesla Stock (TSLA) Despite Dismal Q2 Earnings. Here's Why
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) stock plunged about 13% on Thursday at the time of writing, as investors were disappointed with the company's Q2 earnings miss and elevated capital spending. While several analysts slashed their price targets on TSLA stock, citing margin woes and other headwinds, Bank of America analyst Alexander Perry reiterated a Buy rating on Tesla stock with a price target of $460. Perry lowered his 2026 EPS (earnings per share) estimate to $1.76 from $2.13, citing lower auto and energy gross margins and higher R&D (research and development) spend.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

200% short exposure to TSLA with TSLQ

That said, Perry remains bullish on TSLA stock as he believes that the company is "at the early stages of monetization of its autonomy capabilities."

BofA Analyst Weighs In on Tesla's Q2 Earnings

Perry noted that the Q2 2026 gross margin of Tesla's auto segment was 16.9%, way below the Street's estimate of 19.4% due to a decrease in regulatory credits and lower vehicle ASPs (average selling prices). According to the company, margins would have been roughly flat quarter-over-quarter, excluding the $230 million warranty and tariff benefit recorded in the first quarter. Moreover, Perry noted that costs were higher in Q2 2026 due to an increase in R&D, including pre-production ramp costs for Semi, Optimus, and Cybercab.

On the positive side, Perry highlighted that Tesla reported "record" net new FSD (full self-driving) subscriptions, with total fleet penetration surpassing 15%. Also, the energy generation and storage business fared better than expectations, driven by strong deployments in EMEA (Europe, Middle East, and Africa). Meanwhile, Perry noted that Tesla's robotaxi is now deployed in seven markets (six unsupervised). He thinks that the company is modestly behind its initial target of reaching nine markets by the first half of 2026.

Regarding Optimus humanoid robots, Perry mentioned that production will commence this year on the converted Fremont line, while volumes are expected to be ramped up next year. He added that TSLA emphasized a "flat and long" initial S-curve due to the use of all-new parts and "no supply chain."

Finally, the analyst is bullish about Tesla's autonomous capabilities. "We see autonomous vehicles spurring the next era of mobility and as the most significant change agent in the Auto 2.0 landscape, offering consumers more time, safer travel, and more accessible transportation," said Perry.

Is TSLA Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold Now?

Given ongoing challenges in the EV business and heavy spending, Wall Street has a Hold consensus rating on Tesla stock, with 13 Holds, five Buys, and three Sells. The average TSLA stock price target of $374.30 indicates 15% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: U.S. Initial Jobless Claims Plunge to 57-Year Low
data.description:
U.S. initial jobless claims are at their lowest level since 1969 as the labor market continues to hold strong. That comes despite fears of AI-driven job displacement and rising tensions in the Middle East.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

For the week ended July 18, initial jobless claims fell by 22,000 to 187,000, below the estimate of 210,000.  Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, declined by 2,000 to 1.796 million, also trailing the estimate of 1.809 million. Initial jobless claims serve as a gauge of layoffs, while continuing jobless claims track the number of people receiving unemployment benefits and reflect ongoing unemployment.

Labor Market Holds Up as Fed Eyes Inflation

The data shows that the U.S. labor market remains in a slow-to-fire-and-hire environment. At the same time, one factor that could explain the low initial claims data is a weakening participation rate. Last month, the rate fell to 61.5%, the lowest level in over five years. The rate measures the share of the working-age population that is either employed or actively looking for work.

Inflation remains the key focus within the Fed's dual mandate as the labor market holds up. The central bank is scheduled to hold its next interest rate decision on July 29, where it is likely to hold rates steady, according to the CME FedWatch tool.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Citi Sees 53% Revenue Growth for Palantir in 2027. Here's Why
data.description:
Palantir (PLTR) stock trended lower in Thursday's trading and has fallen 27% year-to-date as investors pulled back from expensive AI stocks. The company is scheduled to report its second-quarter 2026 results on August 3. Ahead of the report, Citi analyst Tyler Radke expects the software company's revenue to grow 53% in 2027, well above the Street's estimate of about 45%. He expects strong growth ahead for Palantir's U.S. commercial business, helped by wider use of its Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) across new industries and regions.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

NVDS: built for a short position on NVDA

Radke maintained a Buy rating on PLTR stock but lowered his price target to $200 from $225 to reflect lower valuation multiples. Still, he believes the stock's recent weakness has made its risk-reward more attractive.

Why Citi Remains Bullish on PLTR Stock

Radke expects Palantir's commercial revenue to grow 67% year-over-year in 2027. His checks with the company and its partners were also positive, which gave him more confidence in his outlook.

The analyst also pointed to recent customer wins in legal services and the neocloud market. Outside the U.S., Palantir recently won a large insurance deal in Mexico. Radke also highlighted Palantir's growing work with Nvidia (NVDA). The two companies are working to bring Nvidia's AI models to U.S. government agencies through Palantir's software.

Beyond that partnership, Radke sees more room for growth in Palantir's government business. He pointed to wider use of its products across the U.S. government. He also believes a USDA contract could support growth in the second half of the year.

While competition is rising, Radke believes Palantir's growing customer base could help it win more business. He sees its wider reach across commercial and government customers as a reason for continued growth. 

Is PLTR Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on PLTR stock based on 15 Buys, four Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average PLTR price target of $182.71 per share implies 50.67% upside potential. (See PLTR Stock Forecast).
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: 'Conquer the Fear,' Says Top Investor About Microsoft Stock
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) investors will be hoping that the company's upcoming fiscal fourth quarter (June quarter) report won't elicit the same reaction from investors as its hyperscaler peer Alphabet did.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The search giant delivered an exceptional Q2 readout but that didn't matter due to one fact: the company hiked its full-year capital expenditure forecast to between $195 billion and $205 billion, up from the $180 billion to $190 billion forecast provided last quarter, reviving worries that financial discipline is being sacrificed in the battle for AI supremacy.

As such, GOOGL shares are taking a hefty beating in Thursday's session, down by 7% as of writing. The contagion has spread across other hyperscalers too, with MSFT stock also down on the news.

So, can we expect more downside when Microsoft reports next Wednesday (July 29)?

That remains to be seen. Nevertheless, looking ahead to the print, top investor Dhierin Bechai takes a positive stance. Despite growing concerns over Microsoft's elevated capital expenditures, declining free cash flow and pressure on margins, Bechai remains bullish on the stock's long-term prospects.

The central issue is not necessarily the size of Microsoft's AI spending, but whether the company is providing investors with enough evidence that those investments are generating attractive returns. Capital expenditures are expected to remain elevated, with a significant portion directed toward GPUs and CPUs that depreciate quickly and may require regular replacement. This creates a legitimate risk that high CapEx becomes a permanent feature of the business.

However, Bechai, who ranks among the top 1% of investors on TipRanks, believes Microsoft has a substantial opportunity to demonstrate that its AI infrastructure investments are creating durable revenue and profit growth. A clearer measure of revenue or earnings generated per dollar of CapEx could help investors look beyond the headline spending figures. Further transparency around infrastructure costs, margins and the potential impact of agentic AI on traditional software subscriptions could also help rebuild investor confidence.

After all, Microsoft remains one of the world's most dominant tech companies, with unmatched exposure to cloud infrastructure, enterprise software, distribution and valuable business data. Bechai believes AI adoption and commercialization remain in their early stages, meaning the company's growth opportunity could still be considerable.

Importantly, Microsoft's valuation has already compressed significantly relative to its historical levels. At approximately 14.1x EV/EBITDA, the stock trades around 28% below its historical median of 19.5x. That suggests some of the concern surrounding Microsoft's elevated spending and broader AI disruption fears may already be reflected in the stock.

In short, Bechai admits that Microsoft's CapEx strategy presents real risks, but nevertheless believes the market could be underestimating the company's long-term AI opportunity.

Although the 5-star investor would welcome greater clarity on how the company is allocating its capital expenditures, Bechai still believes Microsoft remains a Strong Buy. (To watch Bechai's track record, click here)

That is also the conclusion reached by the analyst consensus, a rating based on 35 Buys and 1 Hold and Sell, each. Going by the $558.86 average price target, shares will appreciate by 48% over the next year. (See MSFT stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Canadian Dollar Is the Most Heavily Shorted Currency as Trump Escalates Trade War
data.description:
Canada's dollar has become the most heavily shorted currency as U.S. President Donald Trump escalates his trade war against America's northern neighbor.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Speculators have raised their bearish bets against the Canadian dollar to the highest level among major currencies as U.S. President Donald Trump announced new tariffs on Canada of up to 50% on some goods, helping to push the Canadian currency to ‌a 14-month low.

Short sellers have wagered $12.5 billion that the Canadian dollar has further to fall, according to data from the ‌U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). Trump's proposed 50% tariffs on a wide range of goods are scheduled to take effect on Aug. 19 of this year.

Worse Than the Japanese Yen

Short sellers have ratcheted up their bets against Canada's dollar for two consecutive weeks amid signs of continued economic weakness. Earlier this year, Canada's economy entered a recession defined as two consecutive quarters of contraction. Canada's dollar is now the most shorted currency traded on the Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME).

Canada's dollar has overtaken the Japanese yen as the most widely shorted currency globally. And the Japanese yen fell to its lowest level against the U.S. dollar in 40 years at the end of June. The decline in the Canadian dollar has accelerated since Trump declined to ⁠extend the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement on trade, starting a decade-long wind down of the free trade deal.

Is LULU Stock a Buy?

Let's look at the stock of Lululemon Athletica (LULU), one of Canada's best-known companies. LULU shares have a consensus Hold rating among 21 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on one Buy and 20 Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LULU price target of $167.38 implies 34% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole
